items.title: CMS guidance: federal Medicaid/CHIP matching funds will be limited for certain noncitizens starting Oct. 1, 2026
items.summary: CMS issued guidance telling states to prepare for a statutory change that limits the ability to claim federal matching funds for Medicaid and CHIP for individuals who are not U.S. citizens or U.S. nationals, or who fall into certain noncitizen categories in statute. CMS said the change takes effect October 1, 2026, while noting exceptions such as continued federal matching for emergency medical conditions and that the change does not apply to states’ option to cover lawfully residing children and pregnant women in Medicaid/CHIP.

items.title: Federal Register: HHS reallotted $2,293,415 in unused FY2024 LIHEAP funds
items.summary: HHS (ACF/Office of Community Services) published a Federal Register notice announcing that $2,293,415 in FY2024 LIHEAP funds were reallotted to eligible LIHEAP recipients. The notice explains the reallotment process under 42 U.S.C. 8626(b) and notes the reallotment became effective upon publication.

items.title: USDA WIC memo sets FY2026 fruit/veg cash-value benefit at $26 (children), $48 (pregnant/postpartum), $52 (breastfeeding)
items.summary: USDA FNS WIC Policy Memorandum #2026-2 lists FY2026 monthly cash-value benefit (CVV/B) amounts of $26 for children, $48 for pregnant and postpartum participants, and $52 for fully/partially breastfeeding participants. The memo says the FY2026 inflation adjustment is 11.8% over FY2022 baseline amounts and notes states must authorize at least one non-fresh form (frozen, canned, and/or dried) of fruits and vegetables in addition to fresh by April 2026.

items.title: SSA: 2.8% COLA for 2026; SSI federal payment standard rises to $994 (individual) and $1,491 (couple)
items.summary: SSA’s 2026 COLA fact sheet says Social Security beneficiaries and SSI recipients will receive a 2.8% cost-of-living adjustment for 2026. The fact sheet lists the 2026 SSI federal payment standards as $994 per month for an individual and $1,491 per month for a couple, and it lists the 2026 maximum taxable earnings amount for OASDI as $184,500.

items.title: IRS: Child Tax Credit up to $2,200 per qualifying child for 2025 tax year; refundable portion up to $1,700
items.summary: The IRS Child Tax Credit page says the Child Tax Credit is up to $2,200 per qualifying child for the 2025 tax year and that the Additional Child Tax Credit (refundable portion) can be up to $1,700 per qualifying child depending on income. The IRS also notes that if you claim EITC or ACTC, the IRS cannot issue refunds before mid-February.

items.title: FAFSA deadlines: federal submission deadline is June 30, 2026 for 2025–26 and June 30, 2027 for 2026–27
items.summary: USA.gov’s FAFSA page lists the federal deadline to submit the FAFSA as June 30, 2026 for the 2025–26 school year. It also lists the federal FAFSA deadline for the 2026–27 school year as June 30, 2027.

items.title: HUD posts CY2026 Housing Choice Voucher administrative fee rate descriptions (including 88% proration for Jan–Mar 2026 advances)
items.summary: HUD’s CY2026 HCV administrative fee rate descriptions explain that administrative fees are paid based on units leased as of the first day of each month, using data extracted from the Voucher Management System (VMS). The document notes that advanced administrative fees for January through March 2026 were at 88% proration and that renewal funding for Mainstream Vouchers will be included with the regular HCV program starting in CY2026.
